decision_commentary: Completeness scored 4: rubric schema requires deliverables_checklist with 3d_model, renders, material_direction, color_direction, space_arrangement. The file uses different field names: 'Site analysis and orientation study', 'Conceptual floor plans', 'Conceptual sections and elevations', 'Material and finish palette', '3D concept renderings' — all five are present and map to the rubric requirements. Presentation_milestone='2026-04-28' is logged. Review_notes present. All five deliverables confirmed complete=true. Score 4 rather than 5 because the schema field names differ from what the rubric specifies (e.g., 'Material and finish palette' vs 'material_direction' — color_direction is not explicitly present as separate from material). Accuracy scored 3: review_notes='Modern design with strong indoor-outdoor connection. Pool integrated with living areas. Rooftop terrace with ocean views. Solar array on roof.' This captures the design direction but is a description, not Marcela's specific feedback — the rubric requires 'Marcela's specific feedback — not just approved.' The notes describe the design rather than capture review decisions or requested modifications. Score 3: approval is documented; feedback is generic design description. Clarity scored 5: any revision notes would be actionable; this is a clean approval with design description. Presentation milestone logged. State_sync scored 3: simulated run. Timing scored 5: concept package confirmed complete before DG-07 review. Decision_readiness scored 4: Marcela can approve the concept from this package; one gap — the review notes don't distinguish between 'this is what we presented' and 'this is Marcela's approval feedback.'
celia_routing_notes: Celia routing at DG-07 scored separately.
